title: Controlled Vocabulary: Timeline Transition Change Types
description: Canonical set of state change types used in timeline transitions. Every transition in a timeline moment must use exactly one change type. These describe what happens to a story element at a particular narrative beat — the 'so what' of each moment.

change_types.definition: The entity gains knowledge, understanding, or awareness that they did not previously possess.
change_types.structural_function: Drives Revelation nodes. Creates information asymmetry shifts. What the character knows determines what actions they can take.
change_types.typical_context: Discovering a secret, understanding a truth, receiving instruction, realizing a mistake

change_types.definition: The entity acquires something — an object, ability, ally, status, or resource.
change_types.structural_function: Empowers the entity for future challenges. Often occurs at Catalyst or post-Trial nodes. Marks power increases.
change_types.typical_context: Receiving a gift, winning a prize, earning a title, finding a tool, gaining an ally

change_types.definition: The entity is deprived of something they previously had — an object, person, status, ability, or safety.
change_types.structural_function: Raises stakes and creates vulnerability. Often occurs at Reversal or Descent nodes. Makes subsequent challenges harder.
change_types.typical_context: Theft, death of ally, loss of status, destruction of a tool, exile

change_types.definition: The entity undergoes a fundamental change in nature, identity, appearance, or capability — becoming something different.
change_types.structural_function: Marks the story's core payoff at Transformation nodes. The entity before and after are meaningfully different.
change_types.typical_context: Metamorphosis, moral conversion, identity shift, physical transformation, corruption or purification

change_types.definition: The entity enters a new location, appearing where they were not before.
change_types.structural_function: Marks spatial transitions. Arrival at a new place often triggers new story phases (Threshold, special world entry).
change_types.typical_context: Entering a new realm, arriving at the stronghold, reaching the destination, being transported

change_types.definition: The entity leaves a location, removing their presence from where they were.
change_types.structural_function: Marks departure from a story phase. Leaving the ordinary world, fleeing danger, or being exiled.
change_types.typical_context: Setting out on the journey, fleeing, being banished, choosing to leave

change_types.definition: The entity forms or deepens a relationship with another entity — creating a new connection or strengthening an existing one.
change_types.structural_function: Builds the relationship network. Bonding raises the emotional stakes of future threats to that relationship.
change_types.typical_context: Forming an alliance, falling in love, swearing an oath, earning trust, accepting a mentor

change_types.definition: The entity severs, damages, or fundamentally strains a relationship with another entity.
change_types.structural_function: Drives Reversal and conflict escalation. Broken bonds create isolation and raise the cost of the protagonist's path.
change_types.typical_context: Betrayal, abandonment, disillusionment, choosing one loyalty over another, exile

change_types.definition: The entity ceases to exist — character death, destruction of a place, or irreversible breaking of an object.
change_types.structural_function: The ultimate Irreversible Cost. Death creates permanent stakes and forces surviving characters to continue without the lost entity.
change_types.typical_context: Character killed, place destroyed, object shattered beyond repair, final sacrifice

change_types.definition: A hidden truth about the entity is exposed — to other characters, to the audience, or to the entity itself.
change_types.structural_function: Drives Revelation nodes from the target's perspective. What was hidden becomes known, recontextualizing prior events.
change_types.typical_context: True identity exposed, secret plan discovered, hidden nature made visible, confession

change_types.definition: The entity makes a consequential choice that determines the story's direction — a moment of agency that cannot be undone.
change_types.structural_function: Drives Commitment and Threshold nodes. The decision itself is the transition, not its consequences (which follow as separate transitions).
change_types.typical_context: Accepting the call, choosing sacrifice over safety, selecting a path at a crossroads, committing to a plan
